title: cerastium schizopetalum var. rupicola
intro: Cerastium schizopetalum var. rupicola, also known as the rock cranesbill, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Cerastium schizopetalum var. rupicola include its ability to soothe sore throats and coughs, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or infused in hot water to release its medicinal compounds.
